description: Add server-side socket disconnection for removed agents by maintaining a module-level agentId-to-socketId map, so that when an admin removes an EID the corresponding socket is actively disconnected server-side rather than relying solely on client-side handling.
steps: Add a module-level Map (agentSocketMap) near the Socket.IO setup to track agentId -> socket.id mappings.
In the 'agent-start' socket handler, store the mapping: agentSocketMap.set(agentId, socket.id).
In the 'disconnect' socket handler, remove the mapping: agentSocketMap.delete(socketAgentId).

In the DELETE /api/admin/eids/:eid handler, after saveState, look up the socket from agentSocketMap and call socket.disconnect(true) to force server-side disconnection. Remove the entry from the map. Keep the io.emit('force-stop-agent') as a fallback.
acceptance_criteria: A module-level agentSocketMap exists that maps agentId to socket.id
On agent-start, the mapping is stored
On disconnect, the mapping is removed
On DELETE /api/admin/eids/:eid, the agent's socket is found via the map and disconnected server-side
findings: Added agentSocketMap to track socket connections. On agent removal, the server now actively disconnects the socket rather than relying on client-side handling alone. The fallback broadcast remains for edge cases where the map entry might be stale.
